name: lox_methane_engine
description: Design a minimal LOX/methane liquid rocket engine and size its propellant feeds so the engine reaches a target operating point. The engine burns LOX (oxidizer) and methane/CH4 (fuel). You must hit FOUR coupled requirements at end of burn: thrust in [1500, 2500] N, mixture ratio (MR = mdot_ox/mdot_fuel) in [2.8, 3.6], chamber pressure at least 1.2 MPa, and specific impulse at least 195 s. Thrust and chamber pressure are driven by TOTAL propellant flow; MR is driven by the RATIO of oxidizer-feed to fuel-feed flow. So tune the two feed orifices together: the ox/fuel CdA ratio sets MR, and the overall CdA magnitude sets thrust/Pc. The verdict reports the actual measured thrust, MR, Pc and Isp each run -- use them to converge.

engine_topology: The Engine node MUST have exactly one oxidizer feed and one fuel feed ending at it.
Oxidizer vs fuel is decided by the SOURCE node's fluid: a source whose fluid is Oxygen (or LOX) is the oxidizer feed; the methane source is the fuel feed.
ox_tank: a Node with fluid 'Oxygen' (liquid O2, ~90 K), high pressure.
fuel_tank: a Node with fluid 'Methane' (~110 K), high pressure.
engine: an Engine node named 'engine' with params fuel='CH4', oxidizer='LOX'.
ox_feed: a Connection from ox_tank to engine. fuel_feed: a Connection from fuel_tank to engine.
notes: Good starting point: ox_feed CdA ~ 2-3e-5, fuel_feed CdA ~ 1-1.5e-5.
Larger total CdA -> more flow -> higher thrust and Pc. Higher ox/fuel CdA ratio -> higher MR.
Requirements are checked at end of burn (the 'final' value), which avoids the ignition transient.
Engine sims emit a benign 'engine has unchanged m history' warning -- that is expected and not graded.

checks.description: Simulation runs without invalid-config or solver crash.
End-of-burn thrust is at least 1500 N.
End-of-burn thrust is at most 2500 N.
End-of-burn mixture ratio is at least 2.8.
End-of-burn mixture ratio is at most 3.6 (LOX/CH4 sensible range).
End-of-burn chamber pressure is at least 1.2 MPa.
End-of-burn specific impulse is at least 195 s.
